mechanism_narrative: A persistently low FEDFUNDS rate transmits easier financial conditions into equity valuation via lower discount rates and reduced recession risk, which lifts S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is strongest when inflation is not accelerating.
failure_condition: Reject if low-FEDFUNDS periods do not beat the unconditional 20-day return rate, or if the positive effect disappears when CPIAUCSL inflation is stable or falling.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent level signals weakening household income and corporate revenue expectations, causing S&P 500 returns to turn negative over the next month. The mechanism is a demand-shock warning rather than a valuation effect.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered UNRATE increases are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more often than negative returns, or if the signal vanishes in rolling 3-year tests.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in RSAFS combined with rising HOUST indicates broad final-demand growth and a firming housing cycle, which supports equity earnings expectations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if joint high-RSAFS and high-HOUST states do not improve 60-day forward returns relative to either series alone, or if the combined signal has no positive lift in rolling out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in INDPRO reflects manufacturing slowdown and weaker inventory turnover, which lowers earnings expectations and pressures the S&P 500 over the next month. This is a cyclical production signal that works when demand is not simultaneously expanding.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls by more than 0.5% over 1 month
failure_condition: Reject if monthly INDPRO declines are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in a majority of cases, or if the effect is not distinguishable from noise over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is near the zero lower bound and CPIAUCSL is still firm, equities interpret policy as stuck in accommodation while nominal growth remains intact, supporting positive forward returns. The effect weakens if inflation collapses or policy is tightening.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS < 0.50% and CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation is above 1.5%
failure_condition: Reject if the triggered low-rate/high-inflation state does not produce above-baseline 5-day SPX returns, or if the sign flips negative after controlling for recent market volatility.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in PAYEMS indicates slowing payroll growth and weaker earnings breadth, which reduces risk appetite and lowers S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect is strongest when unemployment is also rising.
failure_condition: Reject if falling PAYEMS does not coincide with negative 20-day SPX returns more often than positive returns, or if the joint PAYEMS-UNRATE signal does not outperform either proxy alone.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in GDPC1 growth relative to the prior quarter signals stronger aggregate demand and improving corporate sales, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter. This works as a slow-moving confirmation signal rather than a fast tactical indicator.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth turns positive after a prior weak quarter
failure_condition: Reject if positive GDP growth turns do not lead to higher 60-day SPX returns than negative or flat GDP transitions, or if the effect disappears after revision-stable vintages are used.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in HOUST and RSAFS marks broad demand retrenchment in housing and retail spending, which is a direct warning of weaker near-term corporate revenue and negative S&P 500 returns. The signal fails when one of the two series remains resilient.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and RSAFS both fall month-over-month in the latest release
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous monthly declines in housing starts and retail sales do not produce negative 5-day SPX returns more often than positive returns, or if the joint pattern is not statistically stronger than either decline alone.
